trivia_bullets.text: Epitaph Golem was first printed in Shadows over Innistrad (April 2016) as an artifact creature inhabiting the gothic horror plane of Innistrad — a plane mechanically defined by its graveyard-matters themes. The golem's ability (returning graveyard cards to library) fits the Innistrad plane's preoccupation with death, memory, and what lingers after burial. [Scryfall: SOI 254]

Flavor text: "Many names and many memories bound together in grim purpose." [Scryfall]. The line frames the golem as a composite entity — assembled from the epitaphs and identities of the dead rather than built from inert stone, consistent with Innistrad's lore of constructs animated by necromantic or spiritual residue.

Art by Adam Paquette across all printings (SOI, M21, Shadows over Innistrad Remastered, Innistrad Remastered). Paquette is known for his atmospheric landscape and environment-forward fantasy illustration; his graveyard scene here — the golem rising over misty headstones under stormy indigo sky — is a strong example of his gothic environmental work. The card has been reprinted four times: SOI (2016), M21 (2020), SIR (2023), INR (2025). [Scryfall print search]

Oracle text: {2}: put target card from your graveyard on the bottom of your library. This is a slow graveyard-to-library recycling effect on a 3/5 body — a colorless 5-drop with no evasion. It is strictly a mill-hate and self-mill-recovery tool, used to loop key cards back through a deck in Commander. EDHREC rank 15,896 reflects niche adoption in mill-prevention or self-mill shells. Legal in Pauper, expanding its reach. [Scryfall]
